_doc: Labelled sample PR diffs encoding the core-store schema migration
convention (migrations/README.md). Each fixture is a real unified diff
against this repo's current src DDL — they apply cleanly with
`git apply --check` from the repo root — and `expect` is the verdict the
schema-migration audit gate (landing separately) must produce for a PR
pass — the gate exits 0 (additive change, artifact shipped, or the
touched DDL is out of scope: Better Auth / extension-owned)
fail — the gate exits non-zero (destructive in-scope change with no
migrations/NNNN_*.sql + manifest entry in the same diff)
The gate's classifier test must run every fixture and assert its label.
When the convention gains a new destructive/additive case, add a fixture
here in the same PR that documents it.

fixtures.description: Drops usage_events.agent_label (CREATE-DDL text, legacy DO-block adds, and an inline DROP COLUMN) without shipping a migrations/ artifact.

Narrows usage_events.cost_usd numeric(12,8) -> numeric(12,4) and ships migrations/0002_narrow-usage-events-cost-usd.sql plus its manifest entry in the same diff (refreshed against the shipped 0001 notifications dedupe-key ledger entry).
Adds a nullable agent_templates.icon column (ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS) and a partial index — no data impact, no artifact required.
Removes a Better Auth additional user field (destructive-shaped, but auth schema is owned by getMigrations and guarded by auth-schema-drift); the gate must ignore it.

fixtures.category: out of scope (extension DDL), bundled with in-scope additive
fixtures.description: Adds a dropColumn op to the extension migration JSON-DSL (emits DROP COLUMN, but extension-owned) bundled with an in-scope additive index; the out-of-scope hunks must not trip the gate.
